'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})

==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

Branch shrines were established in many locations. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})

==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buqqhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buqqhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)" to "|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)"
revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}})

Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|12=draft}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町|12=draft}})

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|12=draft}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町|12=draft}})

==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|12=draft}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間|12=draft}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市|12=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市|12=draft}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺|12=draft}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1859

==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=draft}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市|12=draft}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山|12=draft}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木|12=draft}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
