revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|12=draft}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町|12=draft}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|12=draft}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間|12=draft}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市|12=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市|12=draft}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺|12=draft}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Otori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)|12=draft}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町|12=draft}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=draft}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市|12=draft}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山|12=draft}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木|12=draft}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|12=draft}})

It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).

==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)|12=draft}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町|12=draft}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町|12=draft}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=draft}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市|12=draft}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山|12=draft}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木|12=draft}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|12=draft}})

revision.text:
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ)、'''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ)、'''事比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ)、'''金比羅神社'''（こんぴらじんじゃ）は、香川県琴平町の[[金刀比羅宮]]を総本宮とし、その主祭神である[[大物主|大物主神]]を祀る[[神社]]であり、日本全国に存在する。｢神社」ではなく「宮」と称するものもある。
== 概要 == [[江戸時代]]には船による流通が盛んになると、海運業者や商人によって'''金毘羅信仰'''が日本中に広められ、分社が各地に作られた。明治維新による[[神仏分離]]・[[廃仏毀釈]]によって[[神仏習合]]の金毘羅大権現は廃され、大物主神を主祭神とする神道の神社になった。総本宮の[[金刀比羅宮]]に列して主祭神を大物主神とする金刀比羅神社・琴平神社・金比羅社は日本全国に約600社ある。なお、主祭神を変更せず現在も[[金毘羅権現#金毘羅権現を祀る寺院|金毘羅大権現を祀る社寺]]も存在する。 == 主な金刀比羅神社 == * [[金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[北海道]][[根室市]]琴平町） – 創建1806年 * [[広富金刀比羅神社]] (北海道[[沙流郡]][[日高町 (北海道)|日高町]]広富) * [[金刀比羅神社 (幕別町)|金刀比羅神社]] （北海道[[中川郡 (十勝国)|中川郡]][[幕別町]]猿別） * [[金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[岩手県]][[久慈市]]湊町） – 創建1718年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (秋田市土崎港中央)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[秋田県]][[秋田市]][[土崎港]]中央） * [[金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[常磐関船町]]）
* [[金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[茨城県]][[石岡市]]国府） * [[金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[千葉県]][[流山市]][[上新宿]]） – 創建1823年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[東京都]][[台東区]][[台東 (台東区)|台東]]） – 創建 慶長年間 (1596年 - 1615年) * [[浅川金刀比羅神社]] （東京都[[八王子市]][[初沢町]]） * [[武州青梅金刀比羅神社]] （東京都[[青梅市]]本町） * [[木曽金刀比羅神社]] （東京都[[町田市]]） – 創建 [[傳重寺]]十四世の誉原浄の時代 * [[大綱金刀比羅神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]） * 金刀比羅神社 （神奈川県横浜市[[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]） – [[金刀比羅・大鷲神社]]。創建1859年。 * [[金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[新潟県]][[新潟市]][[中央区 (新潟市)|中央区]][[寄居町 (新潟市)|寄合町]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[東区 (名古屋市)|東区]][[泉 (名古屋市)|泉]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区那古野)|金刀比羅神社]] （愛知県名古屋市[[西区 (名古屋市)|西区]][[那古野]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区菊井)|金刀比羅神社]] （愛知県名古屋市西区[[菊井]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[岐阜県]][[瑞浪市]]釜戸町） * [[金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|金刀比羅神社]] （岐阜県[[恵那市]]山岡町） * [[金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]峰山町） – 創建1811年 * [[大歳金刀比羅神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[多可郡]][[多可町]]中区） – 創建1795年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[和歌山県]][[白浜町]]中） – 創建1836年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[勢見町]]） – 創建1616年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]] （徳島県徳島市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]） – 創建1601年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|金刀比羅神社]] （徳島県[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]） – 創建1601年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|金刀比羅神社]] （徳島県[[阿南市]][[福井町 (阿南市)|福井町]]） * [[金刀比羅神社 (佐賀市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]金立町） * [[金刀比羅神社 (唐津市東城内)|金刀比羅神社]] （佐賀県[[唐津市]]東城内） * [[金刀比羅神社 (長崎市西山)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[長崎県]][[長崎市]]西山）
== 主な金刀比羅宮 ==
* [[鶴形金刀比羅宮]] （秋田県[[能代市]]山神社前） * [[金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|金刀比羅宮]] （東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]][[日本橋 (東京都中央区)|日本橋]]） * [[金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|金刀比羅宮]] （東京都[[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]） – 創建1660年 * [[金刀比羅宮東京分社]] （東京都[[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]） * '''[[金刀比羅宮]]''' （[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]川西） – 総本宮。創建年は諸説。 * [[金刀比羅宮 (三木町)|金刀比羅宮]] （香川県[[木田郡]][[三木町]]平木） * [[金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|金刀比羅宮]] （[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]衣山） * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] （愛媛県松山市新立町） – 創建1966年
== 主な事比羅神社 ==
* [[事比羅神社 (せたな町)|事比羅神社]] （北海道[[久遠郡]][[せたな町]]瀬棚区三本杉） * [[事比羅神社 (坂出市)|事比羅神社]] （香川県[[坂出市]]沙弥） * [[事比羅神社 (鹿島市)|事比羅神社]] （佐賀県[[鹿島市]]浜町）
== 主な琴平神社 == * [[琴平神社 (古平町)|琴平神社]] （北海道[[古平郡]][[古平町]]新地町） – 創建1866年 * [[琴平神社 (三川町)|琴平神社]] （[[山形県]][[東田川郡]][[三川町]]猪子） * [[琴平神社 (久喜市)|琴平神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[久喜市]]太田袋） * [[琴平神社 (青梅市)|琴平神社]] （東京都青梅市梅郷） * [[琴平神社 (川崎市)|琴平神社]] （神奈川県[[川崎市]][[麻生区]][[王禅寺東]]） – 創建は1570年以前であるが金毘羅権現の勧請は1826年。 * [[琴平神社 (上越市)|琴平神社]] （新潟県[[上越市]]中央） – 創建 文化年間 (1804年 - 1818年) * [[琴平神社 (富士宮市万野原新田)|琴平神社]] （[[静岡県]][[富士宮市]]万野原新田） * [[琴平神社 (富士宮市朝日町)|琴平神社]] （静岡県富士宮市朝日町） * [[琴平神社 (豊橋市)|琴平神社]] （愛知県[[豊橋市]]岩田) – 創建1627年 * [[琴平神社 (土佐市)|琴平神社]] （[[高知県]][[土佐市]]高岡町） – 創建1668年以後 * [[琴平神社 (南国市)|琴平神社]] （高知県[[南国市]]里改田） * [[琴平神社 (日高村)|琴平神社]] （高知県[[高岡郡]][[日高村]]本村） * [[琴平神社 (大村市原町)|琴平神社]] （長崎県[[大村市]]原町） * [[別所琴平神社]] （[[熊本県]][[熊本市]][[中央区 (熊本市)|中央区]]琴平本町） – 創建 正徳年間 (1711年 - 1716年)
== 主な金比羅神社・金比羅宮 == * [[金比羅宮 (多摩市)|金比羅宮]] （東京都[[多摩市]][[桜ヶ丘 (多摩市)|桜ヶ丘]]） – 創建 文政年間 (1818年 - 1830年) 中期 * [[金比羅神社 (道志村)|金比羅神社]] （[[山梨県]][[南都留郡]][[道志村]]板橋） * [[金比羅神社 (藤枝市)|金比羅神社]] （静岡県[[藤枝市]]志太） * [[金比羅神社 (羽島市正木町)|金比羅神社]] （岐阜県[[羽島市]]正木町） * [[安井金比羅宮]] （京都府[[京都市]][[東山区]]東大路） – 創建は建治年間 (1275年 - 1277年) であるが金毘羅権現の合祀は1695年。 * [[金比羅神社 (新上五島町有川郷)|金比羅神社]] （長崎県[[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]有川郷） – 創建1754年
== 大物主を祀る他の神社 == *[[金毘羅社 (名古屋市南区)|金毘羅社]] （愛知県名古屋市[[南区 (名古屋市)|南区]][[呼続]]） – [[富部神社]]末社。金毘羅の表記であるが祭神は大物主。 *[[金刀比羅大権現 (西条市)|金刀比羅大権現]] （愛媛県[[西条市]]氷見） – 山里の単独の祠。鳥居の扁額はこのような表記であるが総本宮からの勧請。
== 関連項目 ==
* [[金毘羅権現]] * [[金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)|金毘羅]]
{{神道 横}} [[Category:金刀比羅神社|!ことひらしんしや]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[金刀比羅神社]] to [[Kotohira shrines]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ)、'''琴平神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ)、'''事比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ)、'''金比羅神社'''（こんぴらじんじゃ）は、香川県琴平町の[[金刀比羅宮]]を総本宮とし、その主祭神である[[大物主|大物主神]]を祀る[[神社]]であり、日本全国に存在する。｢神社」ではなく「宮」と称するものもある。
== 概要 == [[江戸時代]]には船による流通が盛んになると、海運業者や商人によって'''金毘羅信仰'''が日本中に広められ、分社が各地に作られた。明治維新による[[神仏分離]]・[[廃仏毀釈]]によって[[神仏習合]]の金毘羅大権現は廃され、大物主神を主祭神とする神道の神社になった。総本宮の[[金刀比羅宮]]に列して主祭神を大物主神とする金刀比羅神社・琴平神社・金比羅社は日本全国に約600社ある。なお、主祭神を変更せず現在も[[金毘羅権現#金毘羅権現を祀る寺院|金毘羅大権現を祀る社寺]]も存在する。 == 主な金刀比羅神社 == * [[金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[北海道]][[根室市]]琴平町） – 創建1806年 * [[広富金刀比羅神社]] (北海道[[沙流郡]][[日高町 (北海道)|日高町]]広富) * [[金刀比羅神社 (幕別町)|金刀比羅神社]] （北海道[[中川郡 (十勝国)|中川郡]][[幕別町]]猿別） * [[金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[岩手県]][[久慈市]]湊町） – 創建1718年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (秋田市土崎港中央)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[秋田県]][[秋田市]][[土崎港]]中央） * [[金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[福島県]][[いわき市]][[常磐関船町]]）
* [[金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[茨城県]][[石岡市]]国府） * [[金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[千葉県]][[流山市]][[上新宿]]） – 創建1823年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[東京都]][[台東区]][[台東 (台東区)|台東]]） – 創建 慶長年間 (1596年 - 1615年) * [[浅川金刀比羅神社]] （東京都[[八王子市]][[初沢町]]） * [[武州青梅金刀比羅神社]] （東京都[[青梅市]]本町） * [[木曽金刀比羅神社]] （東京都[[町田市]]） – 創建 [[傳重寺]]十四世の誉原浄の時代 * [[大綱金刀比羅神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]） * 金刀比羅神社 （神奈川県横浜市[[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]） – [[金刀比羅・大鷲神社]]。創建1859年。 * [[金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[新潟県]][[新潟市]][[中央区 (新潟市)|中央区]][[寄居町 (新潟市)|寄合町]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[東区 (名古屋市)|東区]][[泉 (名古屋市)|泉]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区那古野)|金刀比羅神社]] （愛知県名古屋市[[西区 (名古屋市)|西区]][[那古野]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区菊井)|金刀比羅神社]] （愛知県名古屋市西区[[菊井]]) * [[金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[岐阜県]][[瑞浪市]]釜戸町） * [[金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|金刀比羅神社]] （岐阜県[[恵那市]]山岡町） * [[金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]峰山町） – 創建1811年 * [[大歳金刀比羅神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[多可郡]][[多可町]]中区） – 創建1795年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[和歌山県]][[白浜町]]中） – 創建1836年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[勢見町]]） – 創建1616年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]] （徳島県徳島市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]） – 創建1601年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|金刀比羅神社]] （徳島県[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]） – 創建1601年 * [[金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|金刀比羅神社]] （徳島県[[阿南市]][[福井町 (阿南市)|福井町]]） * [[金刀比羅神社 (佐賀市)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]金立町） * [[金刀比羅神社 (唐津市東城内)|金刀比羅神社]] （佐賀県[[唐津市]]東城内） * [[金刀比羅神社 (長崎市西山)|金刀比羅神社]] （[[長崎県]][[長崎市]]西山）
== 主な金刀比羅宮 ==
* [[鶴形金刀比羅宮]] （秋田県[[能代市]]山神社前） * [[金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|金刀比羅宮]] （東京都[[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]][[日本橋 (東京都中央区)|日本橋]]） * [[金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|金刀比羅宮]] （東京都[[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[虎ノ門]]） – 創建1660年 * [[金刀比羅宮東京分社]] （東京都[[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]） * '''[[金刀比羅宮]]''' （[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]川西） – 総本宮。創建年は諸説。 * [[金刀比羅宮 (三木町)|金刀比羅宮]] （香川県[[木田郡]][[三木町]]平木） * [[金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|金刀比羅宮]] （[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]衣山） * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] （愛媛県松山市新立町） – 創建1966年
== 主な事比羅神社 ==
* [[事比羅神社 (せたな町)|事比羅神社]] （北海道[[久遠郡]][[せたな町]]瀬棚区三本杉） * [[事比羅神社 (坂出市)|事比羅神社]] （香川県[[坂出市]]沙弥） * [[事比羅神社 (鹿島市)|事比羅神社]] （佐賀県[[鹿島市]]浜町）
== 主な琴平神社 == * [[琴平神社 (古平町)|琴平神社]] （北海道[[古平郡]][[古平町]]新地町） – 創建1866年 * [[琴平神社 (三川町)|琴平神社]] （[[山形県]][[東田川郡]][[三川町]]猪子） * [[琴平神社 (久喜市)|琴平神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[久喜市]]太田袋） * [[琴平神社 (青梅市)|琴平神社]] （東京都青梅市梅郷） * [[琴平神社 (川崎市)|琴平神社]] （神奈川県[[川崎市]][[麻生区]][[王禅寺東]]） – 創建は1570年以前であるが金毘羅権現の勧請は1826年。 * [[琴平神社 (上越市)|琴平神社]] （新潟県[[上越市]]中央） – 創建 文化年間 (1804年 - 1818年) * [[琴平神社 (富士宮市万野原新田)|琴平神社]] （[[静岡県]][[富士宮市]]万野原新田） * [[琴平神社 (富士宮市朝日町)|琴平神社]] （静岡県富士宮市朝日町） * [[琴平神社 (豊橋市)|琴平神社]] （愛知県[[豊橋市]]岩田) – 創建1627年 * [[琴平神社 (土佐市)|琴平神社]] （[[高知県]][[土佐市]]高岡町） – 創建1668年以後 * [[琴平神社 (南国市)|琴平神社]] （高知県[[南国市]]里改田） * [[琴平神社 (日高村)|琴平神社]] （高知県[[高岡郡]][[日高村]]本村） * [[琴平神社 (大村市原町)|琴平神社]] （長崎県[[大村市]]原町） * [[別所琴平神社]] （[[熊本県]][[熊本市]][[中央区 (熊本市)|中央区]]琴平本町） – 創建 正徳年間 (1711年 - 1716年)
== 主な金比羅神社・金比羅宮 == * [[金比羅宮 (多摩市)|金比羅宮]] （東京都[[多摩市]][[桜ヶ丘 (多摩市)|桜ヶ丘]]） – 創建 文政年間 (1818年 - 1830年) 中期 * [[金比羅神社 (道志村)|金比羅神社]] （[[山梨県]][[南都留郡]][[道志村]]板橋） * [[金比羅神社 (藤枝市)|金比羅神社]] （静岡県[[藤枝市]]志太） * [[金比羅神社 (羽島市正木町)|金比羅神社]] （岐阜県[[羽島市]]正木町） * [[安井金比羅宮]] （京都府[[京都市]][[東山区]]東大路） – 創建は建治年間 (1275年 - 1277年) であるが金毘羅権現の合祀は1695年。 * [[金比羅神社 (新上五島町有川郷)|金比羅神社]] （長崎県[[南松浦郡]][[新上五島町]]有川郷） – 創建1754年
== 大物主を祀る他の神社 == *[[金毘羅社 (名古屋市南区)|金毘羅社]] （愛知県名古屋市[[南区 (名古屋市)|南区]][[呼続]]） – [[富部神社]]末社。金毘羅の表記であるが祭神は大物主。 *[[金刀比羅大権現 (西条市)|金刀比羅大権現]] （愛媛県[[西条市]]氷見） – 山里の単独の祠。鳥居の扁額はこのような表記であるが総本宮からの勧請。
== 関連項目 ==
* [[金毘羅権現]] * [[金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)|金毘羅]]

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町|qq=draft}})
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kuji|ja|久慈市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuchizaki Port|ja|土崎港|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft}})
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|qq=draft}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間|qq=draft}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市|qq=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市|qq=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市|qq=draft}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺|qq=draft}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町|qq=draft}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市|qq=draft}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山|qq=draft}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木|qq=draft}})
==== Kyushu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Karatsu)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (唐津市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagasaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (長崎市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|qq=draft}})

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nemuro|ja|根室市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft}}) * Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Makubetsu Town|ja|幕別町|qq=draft}})

==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ishioka|ja|石岡市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nagareyama|ja|流山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Minato, Tokyo|ja|港区 (東京都)|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|qq=draft}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間|qq=draft}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hachioji|ja|八王子市|qq=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ome|ja|青梅市|1=Ōme, Tokyo|qq=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Machida|ja|町田市|qq=draft}}, Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺|qq=draft}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kanagawa-ku|ja|神奈川区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区 (横浜市)|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niigata|ja|新潟市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chuo Ward, Niigata|ja|中央区 (新潟市)|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Nagoya|ja|東区 (名古屋市)|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft}} (Nagoya, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Nagoya|ja|西区 (名古屋市)|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mizunami|ja|瑞浪市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ena|ja|恵那市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaoka Town|ja|山岡町|qq=draft}})
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyotango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Shirahama|ja|白浜町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taka District, Hyogo|ja|多可郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町|qq=draft}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Naruto|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft}}, Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Kotohira Shrine (Anan)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Anan|ja|阿南市|qq=draft}}, Tokushima) * {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft}}) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Matsuyama|ja|松山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山|qq=draft}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木|qq=draft}})

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatado District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohiragū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

[[sn:Kotohira shrines]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]
<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine-->

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatado District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohiragū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatado District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohiragū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

[[sn:Kotohira shrines]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]
<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine-->

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatado District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohiragū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

[[sn:Kotohira shrines]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]
<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine-->

'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in {{ill|Kotohira|ja|琴平町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakatado District|ja|仲多度郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatado District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} (abolition of Buddhism) during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, {{ill|Kotohira-gū|lt=Kotohira-gū|lt=Kotohiragū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohiragū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}}, worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still {{ill|Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|lt=Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen#Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaido|Nemuro]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Town|qq=}}) * Konpira Shrine ([[:en:Makubetsu, Hokkaido|Makubetsu Town]])
==== Tohoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (久慈市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Kuji)|qq=}} ([[:en:Kuji, Iwate|Kuji]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) – Founded in 1718 * [[:File:Kotohira-jinja, Tsuchizaki.jpg|Konpira Shrine]] ([[:en:Akita Prefecture|Akita Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsuchizaki Minato, Akita|Tsuchizaki Port]]) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} ([[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]])
==== Kanto Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (石岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Ishioka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ishioka, Ibaraki|Ishioka]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (流山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nagareyama)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) – Founded in 1823 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都中央区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) – Founded in 1660 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Taito)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Taito)|qq=}} – Founded during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長年間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō era|qq=}} (1596–1615) * {{ill|Asakawa Konpira Shrine|ja|浅川金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakawa Konpira Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Hachiōji|Hachioji]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|ja|武州青梅金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bushu Ome Konpira Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Ōme, Tokyo|Ōme, Tokyo]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Kiso Konpira Shrine|ja|木曽金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Konpira Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Machida, Tokyo|Machida]], Tokyo) – Founded during the time of Honen Jō of {{ill|Denjuji|ja|傳重寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Denjuji|qq=}} Temple * {{ill|Otsuna Konpira Shrine|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuna Konpira Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa-ku]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]]) * {{ill|Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku, Yokohama]]) – Founded in 1859
==== Chubu Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新潟市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Niigata)|qq=}} ([[:en:Niigata (city)|Niigata]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Niigata|Chuo Ward, Niigata]]) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市東区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Higashi-ku)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[:en:Higashi-ku, Nagoya|Higashi-ku, Nagoya]]) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (名古屋市西区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nagoya Nishi-ku)|qq=}} (Nagoya, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Nagoya|Nishi-ku, Nagoya]]) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (瑞浪市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Mizunami)|qq=}} ([[:en:Mizunami, Gifu|Mizunami]], {{ill|Kamado Town|ja|釜戸町2510番地の1|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Town|qq=}}) * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ena, Gifu|Ena]], [[:en:Yamaoka, Gifu|Yamaoka Town]])
==== Kinki Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (京丹後市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Kyotango)|qq=}} ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyotango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) – Founded in 1811 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)|qq=}} ([[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]) – Founded in 1836 * {{ill|Otoshi Konpira Shrine|ja|大歳金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshi Konpira Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Taka District, Hyōgo|Taka District, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) – Founded in 1795
==== Shikoku Region ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima]], {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Semimachi|qq=}}) – Founded in 1616 * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|lt=Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|lt=Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Kawauchi)|qq=}} – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}} ([[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto]], Tokushima) – Founded in 1601 * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Anan)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (阿南市)|1=Konpira Shrine (Anan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Anan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Anan)|qq=}} ([[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan]], Tokushima) * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]] ([[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]) – The head shrine; founding date debated * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (松山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Matsuyama)|qq=}} ([[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama]], {{ill|Kinuyama|ja|衣山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinuyama|qq=}}) * Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch (Matsuyama) – Founded in 1966 * Hiraki Kotohira Shrine ({{ill|Miki, Kagawa|ja|木田郡三木町平木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miki, Kagawa|qq=}})

It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaido|Nemuro]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Town|qq=}}) * Konpira Shrine ([[:en:Makubetsu, Hokkaido|Makubetsu Town]])

It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).

Branch shrines were established in many locations. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Konpira Shrines
revision.text:
'''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaido|Nemuro]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Town|qq=}}) * Konpira Shrine ([[:en:Makubetsu, Hokkaido|Makubetsu Town]])

Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Nemuro)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaido|Nemuro]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Town|qq=}}) * Konpira Shrine ([[:en:Makubetsu, Hokkaido|Makubetsu Town]])

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
== Konpira Shrines, Kotohira Shrines, and Konpira Shrines across Japan == === Konpira === ==== Hokkaido ==== * {{ill|Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社 (根室市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nemuro, Hokkaido|Nemuro]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]) – Founded in 1806 * Hirotomi Konpira Shrine ({{ill|Hidaka Town|ja|日高町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Town|qq=}}) * Konpira Shrine ([[:en:Makubetsu, Hokkaido|Makubetsu Town]])

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kotohira shrines]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kotohira shrines]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. <!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

<!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--><!--Kotohira Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kotohira Shrines]] [[simple:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Kotohira shrines]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646488]] Wikidata name: Kotohira shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Konpira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社), also known as '''Kotohira Shrine''' (琴平神社), '''Koto Hira Shrine''' (事比羅神社), or '''Konpira Shrine''' (金比羅神社), is a Shinto shrine dedicated to the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) with its head shrine located in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]], [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. It is a place of worship for the Konpira faith and has many branches across Japan. Some branches use the term "shrine" (神社), while others use "palace" (宮).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Konpira faith spread widely across Japan through the efforts of maritime traders and merchants as shipborne commerce became more common. Branch shrines were established in many locations. Following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) and the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] (abolition of Buddhism) during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the syncretic Konpira Daigongen faith was dismantled. Konpira shrines became Shinto shrines dedicated to Ōmononushi no Kami as their primary deity. There are about 600 shrines nationwide that align with the head shrine, [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohiragū]], worshipping Ōmononushi no Kami. Notably, there are still [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Shrines venerating Konpira Daigongen]] that continue to enshrine Konpira Daigongen without changing the main deity. {{main|Kotohiragū|Konpira Gongen}}
